prompt_inputs.content: Postcolonial theory fundamentally challenges the Western epistemological frameworks that have historically dominated academic discourse. Through examining the power dynamics inherent in colonial narratives, scholars like Said and Spivak interrogate how knowledge production itself becomes a site of resistance and contestation. The subaltern subject, positioned at the margins of colonial discourse, embodies the tensions between agency and subjugation that characterize the postcolonial condition. Furthermore, the concept of hybridity—the blending of colonial and indigenous cultural forms—complicates binary understandings of identity and belonging, revealing how marginalized communities negotiate meaning-making within structures of domination while simultaneously asserting alternative epistemologies rooted in indigenous philosophies and oral traditions.

prompt_inputs.content: This study investigates the role of CRISPR-Cas9 gene editing in modulating autophagy pathways within mammalian cells under hypoxic conditions. We employed liquid chromatography-mass spectrometry to quantify levels of mTOR and AMPK signaling intermediates, revealing a 3.2-fold increase in LC3-II conversion rates following targeted disruption of the ATG7 gene. Furthermore, our immunofluorescence microscopy analysis demonstrated that CRISPR-mediated knockdown of ULK1 resulted in impaired autophagosome formation, with electron microscopy confirming reduced double-membrane vesicle structures. These findings suggest that CRISPR-based therapeutic interventions targeting autophagy regulators may have significant implications for treating lysosomal storage diseases and cancer cell proliferation.

solution_criteria: Extracts distinct topics spanning molecular biology (CRISPR-Cas9, gene editing, autophagy), biochemistry (mTOR, AMPK, LC3-II), analytical methodology (LC-MS, immunofluorescence, electron microscopy), and cellular mechanisms (autophagosome formation, hypoxic conditions)

Returns output as a valid JSON array of strings with no extraneous text or explanations
